task_completion.description: Verify the agent logged into the OpenSIS application as an administrator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenSIS login screen being filled with 'admin' credentials, or the subsequent OpenSIS admin dashboard indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the school calendar or event creation form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenSIS 'School Calendar', 'Add Event', or similar scheduling interface open in the browser.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct event title is entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Annual Science Fair' should be visible in the event title or name input field.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct event date is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The date 'May 20, 2026' or '2026-05-20' should be visible in the event date field or selected on a date picker.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct event description is entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Student project presentations in the gym (1PM - 4PM)' (or at least the keywords 'gym' and 'presentations') should be visible in the event description text area.

task_completion.description: Verify the event is categorized as an Event and not a Holiday.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown, radio button, or checkbox should show the event type set to 'Event', and any 'Holiday' checkbox must be unchecked.

task_completion.description: Verify the event was successfully saved to the calendar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a success confirmation message ('Event added', 'Saved', etc.) or the 'Annual Science Fair' event visibly populated on the calendar view for May 20, 2026.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the OpenSIS graphical user interface to add the event.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must interact with the web browser. There should be no terminal windows open showing curl requests, database SQL inserts, or API scripts used to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent is interacting with the actual OpenSIS web application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar should show a valid local or remote URL for OpenSIS, not a local HTML file (file://) or a text editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually submitted the form rather than just filling it out.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final screenshots must show the state after clicking 'Save' or 'Submit', such as a calendar overview or a success notification, rather than ending the task while still on the filled-out form.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified specifications for the calendar event: Expected Title is 'Annual Science Fair'. Expected Date is May 20, 2026 (2026-05-20). Expected Description must contain the keywords 'gym' and 'presentations'. The event must be marked as a regular Event type, and the 'should_be_holiday' setting must be false.
